_doc: Hand-editable target measure for emission-v1 diversity. Uniform over feasible cells (type × morph_cluster × palette_flavor × render_style) by default. SCHEMA (as the loader emission.cells.TargetMeasure actually parses it — verified campaign-2 preflight): each weight_overrides entry is {"match": {axis: [values...]}, "weight": M} and multiplies the base weight (1.0) of every cell whose value on each named axis is in that axis's listed set. A flat {"fractal_type": [...], "multiplier": M} form is SILENTLY IGNORED (weight() reads ov['match'] / ov['weight']), degenerating the measure to uniform. A match may also key on the non-cell axis "source_tag" (the durable ledger intake tag, e.g. classic_phoenix): the emission loader resolves it to concrete morph clusters per intake (TargetMeasure.resolve_source_tags) so config never pins unstable cluster ids; an unresolved consumer (the discovery-side per-type projection) treats source_tag as a never-matching axis, i.e. a no-op. attempt_cap evicts a cell from the target support after that many colorize attempts with zero fills. softmax_temp controls how sharply the deficit-max option is preferred (small → argmax, large → uniform); deficits are range-normalized before softmax so this is scale-free.
_weights_note: First hand-placed allocation order, post campaign-1 blind read (2026-07-20). Rationale: julia families up-weighted on looks-scarcity (the proven vein, under-produced; per-twin census ordering NOT baked in — bug-survivor rates, re-measured at campaign 2, differentiate then if warranted); mandelbrot slight up (best c-plane family); mb3/mb5 slightly down; mb4 token weight, not zero (worst to humans, but nonzero + attempt caps lets the fixed dup metric prove or disprove a yield change). Only relative size matters. Implied per-type shares IF the projection normalizes types independently of cluster count: each julia twin ~19% (julia total ~77%), mandelbrot ~9%, mb3/mb5 ~6% each, mb4 ~2%.
_phoenix_note: Phoenix entered the library at library_intake_2 (2026-07-21, t_good 0.45): 196 varied (grid) + 41 classic (Ushiki) distinct clusters. Classic phoenix separates CLEANLY in morph space (its clusters share zero members with the varied grid clusters). TWO-KNOB control, because overrides MULTIPLY and both phoenix rows are fractal_type=='phoenix': (1) the fractal_type:['phoenix'] BUDGET knob (0.21) sets the family's overall share — a narrow log-spiral depth-vein (docs/findings/phoenix_grid_labels.md), not a breadth family, so it is held to ~7% of the joint measure (was ~22% unweighted, dominated by the 196 varied clusters); (2) the source_tag:['classic_phoenix'] SPLIT knob (1.9) is the classic-vs-varied multiplier that STACKS on the budget knob (classic cell weight = 0.21*1.9), tuned so classic lands ~2% and varied ~5% of the release. Only relative sizes matter; the joint share depends on all weights + live cluster counts, so re-tune at emission. The SPLIT knob keys on the durable ledger source tag (mix_source=='classic_phoenix'), NOT on morph_cluster ids: the emission loader (cells.TargetMeasure.resolve_source_tags) resolves the tag to whatever clusters those 41 classic locations currently occupy at intake, so a re-cluster can never silently invalidate it. Equivalence at first_release intake: the classic-tagged set == the old phoenix#196..236 cluster set exactly, so this is behavior-identical to the retired cluster-id form.
